title: State-changing backward goto loops
problem: The script `set N=0; :loop; set /a N=N+1; if %N% LSS 3 goto loop; echo %N%` currently deobfuscates to `echo 1` and emits GotoUnresolved for `loop`. The interpreter rejects every backward goto after a target has been visited, even though it already has output-elision and hard-loop caps.
counterexample: A backward polling loop that never changes state must still fall through to later static IOCs instead of consuming the full loop budget.
